Current Operations

    Other Operations

    Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • USS Gerald R. Ford to Return to Crete Amid Iran Operation - Reports Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- The USS Gerald R. Ford aircraft carrier, currently deployed in the Middle East, is set to return next week to a NATO naval base on Crete, Greek newspaper eKathimerini reported on Tuesday, citing a source.
  • About 200 U.S. troops injured since start of U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran: command People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- About 200 U.S. troops have been injured since the United States and Israel launched massive attacks on Iran more than two weeks ago, a spokesperson with the U.S. Central Command said on Monday.
  • U.S. continues strikes to curb Iran's threat in Strait of Hormuz: commander People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- The U.S. forces are continuing operations to weaken Iran's ability to threaten commercial shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, Brad Cooper, head of U.S. Central Command, said Monday.

  • Effective Leader of the IRGC, Ali Larijani, Has Been Eliminated Israel Defense Forces 17 Mar 2026 -- Today, the IDF eliminated Ali Larijani in a precise overnight strike near Tehran. The operation was carried out by the IAF, based on IDF intelligence and advanced operational capabilities, targeting him as the de facto leader of the Iranian regime.
  • Gholamreza Soleimani, Commander of the Basij Unit, Eliminated Israel Defense Forces 17 Mar 2026 -- The IDF's strike eliminated Gholamreza Soleimani, the commander of Iran's Basij forces, delivering a major blow to the regime's military leadership. The operation targeted key elements of Iran's armed forces, significantly weakening its command and control structure.
  • Statement by Prime Minister Netanyahu Israel - Prime Minister 17 Mar 2026 -- This morning, we eliminated Ali Larijani. Ali Larijani is the boss of the Revolutionary Guards, that group of gangsters that effectively runs Iran. Alongside him, we also eliminated the commander of the Basij"
  • FM Sa'ar holds a press conference in Jerusalem with Estonian FM Margus Tsahkna Israel MFA 17 Mar 2026 -- FM Sa'ar: ""Last night, the IDF took out Commander of the Basij Soleimani and his deputy, and Secretary of the Supreme National Security Council Ali Larijani. The Iranians are safer without them."

  • True Promise 4: Iran and resistance axis ops. against US-Israeli assets on Mar. 17 Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Dr. Ali Larijani, a seasoned statesman, philosopher and political strategist of the Islamic Republic of Iran, attained martyrdom late on Monday in an American-Israeli act of aggression.
  • IRGC chief: Defenders of Persian Gulf islands fully ready for any aggression Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- During a visit to islands in the Persian Gulf, Commander of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) Ground Forces Brigadier General Mohammad Karami said forces stationed there are fully prepared to confront any scenario.
  • 59th wave of strikes: IRGC debuts 'Haj Qassem' missiles in fierce retaliation Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched a new barrage in the 59th wave Operation True Promise 4, deploying the advanced "Haj Qassem" missile for the first time.
  • Iran: Over 67,000 civilian units damaged after 18 days of US-Israeli aggression Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Red Crescent Society (IRCS) has released figures on damage to civilian infrastructure following US-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic, reporting more than 67,000 civilian units and 498 schools affected by enemy strikes.
  • IRGC announces start of 'effect-based' strikes as Army targets Israeli-US strategic assets Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps has announced a new phase of retaliatory operations against US and Israeli targets as Iran's Army strikes key bases of the invading coalition.
  • Iran's wartime performance to deter any renewed aggression: Army spokesman Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Army spokesman says the Islamic Republic should defend itself in a way that would deter any renewed aggression against the country in the future.
  • Iran launches 58th wave of Op. True Promise 4, pounding enemy targets with precision missiles Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched its massive 58th wave of retaliatory missile and drone strikes against Israeli positions and United States military bases.
  • Iranian missiles strike near Netanyahu's office in massive retaliatory barrage Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- A new wave of Iranian missiles has pounded the occupied territories, with projectiles reportedly striking near from the office of Zionist regime Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu in occupied al-Quds.
  • Iran Attacks Assets of Israeli Arms Maker Rafael Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian military attacked production facilities belonging to the Israeli arms manufacturer Rafael on Tuesday, the Iranian army said.
  • Iran strikes heart of occupied territories, US base in Qatar in 57th wave of retaliation Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has launched the 57th wave of Operation True Promise 4, striking strategic targets deep within the occupied territories and a major US air base in Qatar, according to a statement from the Khatam al-Anbiya Central Headquarters.

  • Iraq's resistance vows to continue fight until occupiers are expelled Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Iraqi Islamic resistance group Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba says the resistance will continue until the complete expulsion of occupying forces and the restoration of the country's full sovereignty.
  • Fire erupts at U.S. embassy in Baghdad after attack People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- Facilities inside the U.S. Embassy compound in Baghdad's Green Zone caught fire after the embassy was attacked early Tuesday.
  • US embassy in Iraqi capital comes under drone strike Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The US embassy in the so-called Green Zone of the Iraqi capital Baghdad has come under a drone strike, with no immediate reports about possible casualties and the extent of damage caused.

  • US Was Unprepared for Scale of Iranian Drone Threat - Reports Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- The United States was not prepared for the scale of an Iranian drone threat it has faced in the region after launching a military campaign against the Islamic Republic, CNN reported on Tuesday, citing sources.
  • Trump Says US No Longer Needs NATO Assistance With Iran Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- The United States no longer needs nor desires the assistance of NATO member states to secure the Strait of Hormuz amid the conflict with Iran, President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, claiming that Washington never required help.
  • Trump issues broadside against NATO allies for Iran war refusal Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Mar 2026 -- President Donald Trump lashed out at NATO allies on Tuesday for refusing to join Washington's military operation against Iran, claiming that the US does not need the help of any country, Anadolu Agency reports.
  • US Struggles to Build Hormuz Coalition as Allies Hold Back Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- US allies have not yet agreed to join the coalition for securing navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, the Axios news portal has reported, citing sources familiar with discussions.
  • Military Officials Present Trump With Options for Ending War With Iran - Reports Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- Military officials have presented US President Donald Trump with options for ending the conflict with Iran should the American leader decide to do so, the NBC News broadcaster reported, citing sources.
  • US Intelligence Sees No Rift in Iran's Leadership - Reports Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- US intelligence has not detected any divisions within Iran's leadership following the launch of the military operation against the country, The Washington Post reported on Tuesday, citing sources familiar with the matter.
  • Top US 'counterterrorism' official resigns, says 'cannot in good conscience' back war on Iran Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Joseph Kent, the Trump administration's director of the National Center for Counterterrorism (NCC), has resigned in protest over the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • Top US Counterterrorism Official Resigns in Protest of Operation Against Iran Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- Joseph Kent on Tuesday announced his decision to step down as director of the US National Counterterrorism Center (NCTC) because of his disapproval of the US military operation against Iran.
  • US official rejects FT report, says any delay in Trump's China visit not tied to request for China to help reopen Hormuz Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Monday rejected a Financial Times report that Trump threatened to delay his China visit if Beijing does not assist the Strait of Hormuz escort effort, saying that US President Donald Trump's visit to China could be delayed if he chooses to stay in Washington due to war on Iran.

  • Iran Confirms Security Chief Larijani, Paramilitary Commander Soleimani Killed RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- Iranian authorities have confirmed that the country's powerful security chief, Ali Larijani, has been killed, the highest profile official to die since Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei was killed on the first day of US and Israeli air strikes on February 28.
  • Ali Larijani: Iran's Key Power Broker And Canny Operator RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- Ali Larijani, one of Iran's most powerful figures and a close confidant of the late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, was killed in an air strike in the capital, Tehran, on March 17.
  • Obituary: Dr. Ali Larijani, a man for all seasons who served Islamic Republic until last breath Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- By Press TV Website Staff
  • IRGC mourns martyrdom of Iran's top security official, vows definite revenge Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has mourned the martyrdom of Iran's top security official during unprovoked American-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic, pledging to avenge the atrocity.
  • Iranian officials mourn martyrdom of Larijani in US-Israeli attack, vow to avenge his blood Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Top Iranian officials have paid glowing tributes to the martyred top security official of the country, Dr. Ali Larijani, in the American-Israeli aggression.
  • Iran's top security official, Dr. Ali Larijani, martyred in American-Israeli aggression Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The secretary of Iran's Supreme National Security Council (SNSC) has ascended to martyrdom in the ongoing American-Israeli aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • US-Israeli 'cowardly' strike assassinates Iran's Basij commander: Statement Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the martyrdom of the head of the Basij forces, Major General Gholamreza Soleimani, in a targeted strike by the American-Zionist enemy.
  • IRGC Aerospace Force commander vows to break the enemy's back on battlefield Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- The commander of the Aerospace Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has announced the launch of a fresh wave of rapid strikes against the United States' assets and the Israeli-occupied territories.
  • US-Israeli joint aggression against Iran disrupts transit in Strait of Hormuz: FM Araghchi Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said that recent disruptions to maritime navigation in the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz are a direct consequence of joint US-Israeli military aggression against the country.
  • Dena martyrs make Army's foundation 'firmer', says Larijani as Tehran prepares for massive funeral Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- As the Iranian capital gears up for a massive funeral honoring 84 martyred sailors of the IRIS Dena, top officials declared that the cowardly assassination of these naval heroes will only make the foundation of the country's armed forces "firmer for years."
  • Qalibaf: Threat of war against Iran, region must be eliminated Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says threat of war against Iran and the region must be eliminated, stressing that Tehran will no longer accept the cycle of war, ceasefire, negotiation, and war again.
  • Araghchi rebukes Hegseth's moral bankruptcy, ignorance of armed conflict law Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has lashed out at US Secretary of War Pete Hegseth for threatening Iran with merciless strikes, saying the threats denote his moral bankruptcy and ignorance about the law of armed conflict.
  • President Pezeshkian: 'Iran will not surrender to bullies' Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says Iran "will not surrender to bullies," urging the international community to condemn the US-Israeli act of aggression and to compel the perpetrators to respect international law.
  • Qalibaf: We did not expect attacks on Iran to originate from southern neighbors Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Parliament Speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf says Tehran did not expect its southern neighbors' territories being used for aggression against the Islamic Republic.

  • Iran confiscates large number of Starlink satellite systems Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Iran's Intelligence Ministry has discovered and confiscated a considerable number of Starlink satellite systems sent by the United States and Israel.
  • Iran Seizes Hundreds of Banned Starlink Devices Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- The Iranian Intelligence Ministry said on Tuesday that it had seized hundreds of Starlink satellite internet devices that it believes were sent to the Islamic Republic by the United States and Israel.

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the Middle East UN Secretary-General 17 Mar 2026
  • WFP projects food insecurity could reach record levels as a result of Middle East escalation WFP 17 Mar 2026 -- The United Nations World Food Programme (WFP) is warning today that the total number of people around the world facing acute levels of hunger could reach record numbers in 2026 if the escalation in the Middle East continues to destabilize the world's economy.
  • Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa, Edouard Beigbeder, on the killing of Palestinian children in the West Bank UNICEF 17 Mar 2026 -- "Violence has again claimed the lives of Palestinian children in the West Bank. On 14 March, two siblings, aged 5 and 7, were shot and killed by Israeli forces in the town of Tammun in the West Bank alongside their parents inside their car.
  • Middle East war risks pushing 45 million more people into acute hunger UN News 17 Mar 2026 -- The Middle East war could cause the worst disruption to lifesaving humanitarian work since COVID, the UN World Food Programme (WFP) warned on Tuesday, as the UN chief again demanded an end to the widening conflict.
  • UN warns of mounting humanitarian threat amid Mideast conflict People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- Warning of the mounting threat to civilians and infrastructure across the Middle East from the regional conflict, UN humanitarians said Monday they are ready to assist national authorities in response.

  • China to offer emergency humanitarian aid to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon, Iraq Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- China has decided to offer emergency humanitarian assistance to Iran, Jordan, Lebanon and Iraq in the hope of easing the humanitarian plight faced by local people, Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Tuesday.

  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Four Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 17 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Three Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 17 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: A Ballistic Missile Launched Toward Al-Kharj Governorate Intercepted and Destroyed SPA 17 Mar 2026
  • Ministry of Defense Spokesperson: Seven Drones Intercepted and Destroyed in Eastern Region SPA 17 Mar 2026
  • Interior Minister Discusses Regional Developments with Omani Counterpart SPA 17 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Interior Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz held a phone call today with Omani Minister of Interior Sayyid Hamoud bin Faisal Al Busaidi, during which they discussed regional developments in light of the heinous Iranian aggression targeting the security and stability of Gulf Cooperation Council countries and the region
  • Foreign Minister, Algerian Counterpart Discuss Regional Developments SPA 17 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Foreign Affairs Prince Faisal bin Farhan bin Abdullah received a phone call from Algerian Minister of State, Minister of Foreign Affairs, National Community Abroad and African Affairs Ahmed Attaf, during which they discussed the latest regional developments and the ongoing efforts to address them
  • Saudi, Qatari Interior Ministers Discuss Regional Security and Reaffirm Commitment to Enhanced Cooperation SPA 17 Mar 2026 -- Minister of Interior Prince Abdulaziz bin Saud bin Naif bin Abdulaziz held a phone call today with Qatari Minister of Interior and Commander of the Internal Security Force Sheikh Khalifa bin Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, during which they discussed regional security developments and the current situation regarding the blatant Iranian attacks targeting the Gulf Cooperation Council countries and the region
  • HRH the Crown Prince, Egyptian President Review Current Developments, Discuss Serious Military Escalation SPA 17 Mar 2026 -- His Royal Highness Prince Mohammed bin Salman bin Abdulaziz Al Saud, Crown Prince and Prime Minister, received a phone call from President of the Arab Republic of Egypt Abdel Fattah El-Sisi, during which they reviewed the current developments and discussed the serious military escalation taking place in the region

  • "A destructive war, in which Israel acts as the battering ram, is unfolding in our region" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 17 Mar 2026 -- Speaking at an iftar dinner with journalists and writers, President Erdoğan said: "A destructive war, in which Israel acts as the battering ram, is unfolding in our region at the moment. Innocent children are being murdered brutally as they are attending classes in their schools. A network, which holds power and deems themselves superior to other people, is dragging our region into a disaster step by step."
  • Foreign Secretary statement on the Middle East conflict: 17 March UK FCDO 17 Mar 2026 -- The Foreign Secretary made a statement to Parliament on the conflict in the Middle East.
  • Iran's Oil Exports Uninterrupted Despite US-Israeli Operation - Energy Commission Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- Iran's oil exports are proceeding without disruption amid the conflict with the United States and Israel, Ismail Hosseini, spokesman for the Iranian Parliament Commission on Energy, said on Tuesday.
  • Prime Minister Speaks with UAE President India PIB 17 Mar 2026 -- The Prime Minister spoke with HH Sheikh Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, President of the UAE, and conveyed advance Eid greetings
  • Japan-Iran Foreign Ministers' Telephone Call Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Mar 2026
  • European allies resist Trump's call for military mission in Strait of Hormuz People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- Multiple European nations and the European Union (EU) on Monday voiced reluctance or outright opposition to U.S. President Donald Trump's call for a military mission to ensure safe shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • HE GCCSG Condemns in the Strongest Terms the Continued Treacherous Iranian Attacks on the Brotherly UAE and Other Council States Gulf Cooperation Council 17 Mar 2026
  • Russia: US-Israeli attack on Iran unreasonable, treacherous Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Russia has condemned the ongoing US-Israeli military attacks on Iran as unreasonable and treacherous, more than two weeks after the invading coalition began an unprovoked war of aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • German chancellor rules out military participation in Strait of Hormuz mission People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz on Monday ruled out any military involvement in protecting oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz, emphasizing that the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) is a "defense alliance" rather than an "intervention alliance."

  • Amid US-Israeli Campaign, Iranian Kurds Watch And Wait RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- After initially voicing support, US President Donald Trump says he doesn't want Iran's Kurds to enter the military operation against Tehran -- at least for now. Still, one group of exiled Kurds is "preparing for war" just in case, according to a photographer who visited one stronghold of Iranian Kurds.

  • Cartoons For Kids, Cash For Iran, Weapons For Houthis: A Web Woven In Moscow RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- Early in 2022, a cartoon series called Bunny Brothers aired on a state-run Russian TV channel whose programming is geared toward young children.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • War Department Senior Leaders Prioritize Western Hemispheric Security DoD News 17 Mar 2026 -- The War Department is prioritizing security in the Western Hemisphere, which is integral to U.S. homeland defense, said Joseph M. Humire, performing the duties of assistant secretary of war for homeland defense and Americas security affairs, who spoke today during a House Armed Services Committee hearing in Washington.
  • National Guard Airlift Wing Completes 38th Year of Antarctic Support DoD News 17 Mar 2026 -- The New York Air National Guard's 109th Airlift Wing concluded its 38th year supporting American scientific research in Antarctica during the 2025-2026 Operation Deep Freeze season, March 11.
  • Air Force Research Laboratory Tests Draper Liquid Rocket Engine in Flight DoD News 17 Mar 2026 -- The Air Force Research Laboratory and an aerospace technology and defense systems contractor recently flew the Affordable Rapid Missile Demonstrator, powered by the Draper liquid rocket engine, achieving supersonic speeds and demonstrating key concepts of operations. This flight is an innovative step toward advancing capabilities that strengthen deterrence and provide rapid global strike options.
  • 419th Fighter Wing Reservists support first-ever microreactor airlift US Air Force 17 Mar 2026 -- When a C-17 Globemaster III touched down at Hill Air Force Base, Feb. 15, it carried more than cargo, it delivered a glimpse of how the military may power future operations.
  • Space Force announces second tranche of PAEs United States Space Force 17 Mar 2026 -- In line with the ongoing Department of War-driven Acquisition Transformation, the Space Force has designated its second tranche of mission areas that will be assigned Portfolio Acquisition Executives, including: Infrastructure; Battle Management, Command, Control, Communication and Space Intelligence; Satellite Communication and Positioning, Navigation and Timing; and Missile Warning and Tracking.

  • Allied naval forces train for high intensity operations in Arctic exercises NATO Allied Maritime Command 17 Mar 2026 -- Standing NATO Maritime Group One (SNMG1) participated in Cold Response 2026, one of the Alliance's major training exercises in the High North, conducted from 9 - 19 March under Norwegian leadership and within the framework of the enhanced vigilance activity Arctic Sentry.
  • NATO Allies continue activities and surveillance along the Eastern Flank, enhancing Integrated Air and Missile Defence and Counter-UAS NATO Allied Air Command 17 Mar 2026 -- NATO Allied Air Command (AIRCOM) recently conducted targeted Integrated Air and Missile Defence (IAMD) training in Romania under eVA Eastern Sentry. The exercise, executed as a Flexible Deterrent Option (FDO), was designed to strengthen deterrence and enhance operational readiness along the Alliance's Eastern Flank.
  • NATO and the European Union discuss stability in the Western Balkans NATO 17 Mar 2026 -- On Tuesday (17 March 2026), the NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska welcomed Ambassador Peter Sorensen, the EU Special Representative for the Belgrade-Pristina Dialogue, to NATO Headquarters.
  • Joint statement on Cold Response 26 and NATO's Forward Land Forces in Finland by the Ministers of Defence of Finland, Sweden and Norway Government of Norway 17 Mar 2026 -- Joint statement on Cold Response 26 and NATO's Forward Land Forces in Finland by the Ministers of Defence of Finland, Sweden and Norway on 16 March 2026.

  • Russia's MiG-31 Fighters With Kinzhal Missiles Fly Over Sea of Japan Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- Russia's MiG-31 fighters armed with Kinzhal hypersonic aeroballistic missiles have performed a scheduled flight over the neutral waters of the Sea of Japan, the Russian Defense Ministry said on Tuesday.

  • UK-Ukraine joint statement: Enhanced Security and Defence Industrial Collaboration Declaration Gov.UK 17 Mar 2026
  • The UK-Ukraine Strategic Dialogue Gov.UK 17 Mar 2026 -- On 17 March 2026 United Kingdom Prime Minister Keir Starmer and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met to discuss the UK-Ukraine Strategic Dialogue.
  • Ukraine Defence Partnership set to boost global production and national security Gov.UK 17 Mar 2026 -- The UK and Ukraine are set to agree a new world-leading partnership to boost global defensive capability against the the proliferation of low cost, high tech military hardware, including drones.
  • Ministry of Defence launches first AI center of excellence to advance AI integration in defense Ukraine MOD 17 Mar 2026

  • 10th China-Vietnam Border Defense Friendship Exchange Kicks off PRC MND 17 Mar 2026 -- The Vietnamese naval taskforce arrived at the port of Fangchenggang in south China's Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region on March 16, as the 10th China-Vietnam Border Defense Friendship Exchange kicked off.

  • Minister Wellington Koo Li-hsiung Continues to Strengthen ROC Armed Forces Combat Power and Enhance Defense Resilience ROC MND 17 Mar 2026
  • Black box of missing F-16 retrieved after long search: Air Force CNA 17 Mar 2026 -- Taiwan's military has found and retrieved the flight data recorder (FDR) of an F-16V fighter jet that disappeared off eastern Taiwan in January and will soon send it to its manufacturer in the United States to decode the data.

  • Operationalising the Australia-New Zealand Alliance: Anzac 2035 - Closer Defence Relations Statement New Zealand Government 17 Mar 2026 -- [Joint Statement delivered at the Australia - New Zealand Foreign and Defence Ministerial Consultations, Canberra 17 March 2026]
  • Joint Statement Australia-New Zealand Foreign and Defence Ministerial Consultations 2+2 New Zealand Government 17 Mar 2026
  • Australia-New Zealand Foreign and Defence Ministerial Consultations (ANZMIN 2+2) Australia DOD 17 Mar 2026 -- Today we will host New Zealand Foreign Minister Winston Peters MP and Defence Minister Judith Collins KC MP for our annual Australia-New Zealand Foreign and Defence Ministerial Consultations (ANZMIN 2+2) in Canberra.
  • Developing excellence across warfighting domains Australia DOD 17 Mar 2026 -- Through the seamless integration of advanced capabilities and joint military assets, Exercise Diamond Seas 26 strengthens Australia's ability to train, test and prepare the next generation of integrated air power professionals.
  • Complex naval manoeuvres at sea Australia DOD 17 Mar 2026 -- The Royal Australian Navy continues to strengthen maritime readiness through officer-of-the-watch manoeuvres with regional partner nations in waters off Australia's northern coast during Exercise Kakadu.
  • RNZAF working hand-in-hand with Australian counterparts New Zealand Defence Force 17 Mar 2026 -- For the first time a Royal New Zealand Air Force (RNZAF) C-130J Hercules has been flying under an Australian call sign, while being embedded in a Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF) squadron.

  • Minister Zůna's address in the "Our Security Cannot be Taken for Granted" Conference Czech Republic Ministry of Defence & Armed Forces 17 Mar 2026 -- The Prague Castle was the venue to the 13th edition of the conference titled "Our Security cannot be taken for Granted".

  • Finland participating in discussions on new financing mechanism for defence Finnish MoD 17 Mar 2026 -- Finland and a group of other NATO countries are exploring the establishment of a new financing mechanism.

  • Minister Budrys in Zagreb: Croatian soldiers in Lithuania is an example of solidarity between allies Lithuania MFA 17 Mar 2026 -- On 17 March, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Lithuania, Kęstutis Budrys, is paying a working visit to Croatia.

  • PH needs to beef up air defenses - defense chief Philippine News Agency 17 Mar 2026 -- The Philippines needs to beef up its air defenses, especially in weapon systems that are capable of intercepting missiles fired by hostile nations.
  • DND chief: No '3rd Force' coup threat detected Philippine News Agency 17 Mar 2026 -- Department of National Defense Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday said no threats have been detected from a so-called "Third Force" which is reportedly plotting to seize power and form a civilian-military junta.
  • Teodoro bats for creation of strategic oil reserves for military Philippine News Agency 17 Mar 2026 -- Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday pushed for the creation of strategic oil reserves and accompanying facilities for the military.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for March 17, 2026
  • W International Companies Agree to Pay $10.5M to Settle False Claims Act Allegations for Overcharging the Air Force and the Navy for Weld Tables US Dept. of Justice 17 Mar 2026 -- The Justice Department today announced that W International LLC, W International SC LLC, Precision Metal Equipment Handling LLC, and Edward Walker (collectively, "Defendants") have agreed to pay $10.5 million to resolve allegations under the False Claims Act that they knowingly overcharged the U.S. Air Force and the U.S. Navy for weld tables.

  • Israel's settlement expansion drives mass displacement in West Bank - UN report OHCHR 17 Mar 2026 -- The Government of Israel has accelerated unlawful settlement expansion and annexation of large parts of the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem, forcibly displacing over 36,000 Palestinians amid increasing violence by Israeli security forces and settlers, says a UN Human Rights Office report.
  • Hezbollah continues fighting Israeli forces, targets more invading tanks Press TV 17 Mar 2026 -- Lebanon's Hezbollah resistance movement is engaged in intense fighting with Israeli forces on the Lebanese border with the occupied territories amid Tel Aviv's ongoing violations of the country's sovereignty.

  • Today's top news: Middle East, Occupied Palestinian Territory, Cuba UNOCHA 17 Mar 2026

  • 1990 letter on Scarborough Shoal 'not binding' - DND chief Philippine News Agency 17 Mar 2026 -- Department of National Defense (DND) Secretary Gilberto Teodoro Jr. on Tuesday described as "not binding" the letter presented by China alleging that Scarborough Shoal (Bajo de Masinloc) is not part of Philippine territory.

  • Afghanistan: Deadly blast kills many at rehab center in Kabul OHCHR 17 Mar 2026
  • Joint statement by High Representative/Vice President Kallas and Commissioner Lahbib on Kabul rehabilitation centre strike European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Mar 2026 -- The strike on a medical facility in Kabul is another deadly escalation in a conflict that needs to end as soon as possible.
  • 'No matter what', UN Women vows to stay in Afghanistan, amid deepening rights crisis UN News 17 Mar 2026 -- UN Women will continue delivering for Afghan women and girls despite sweeping restrictions and ongoing instability, a senior official said on Tuesday.

  • Afghanistan: UN condemns deadly attack on rehab centre in Kabul UN News 17 Mar 2026 -- Top UN officials condemned on Tuesday Pakistan's overnight strike on a rehab centre that reportedly killed at least 400 people in Kabul, according to Taliban authorities, and injured more than 250 others.
  • Afghan Taliban Says Hundreds Killed In Pakistani Air Strike On Kabul Hospital RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- At least 400 people were killed and 250 injured in a Pakistani air strike on a hospital in Kabul, the Afghan Taliban said, while Pakistan rejected the claims.
  • New Round of Escalation Between Pakistan and Afghanistan Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- On Monday, Pakistani drones targeted multiple sites in Kabul, including a 2,000-bed drug rehabilitation center in the eastern part of the Afghan capital. Zabihullah Mujahid, spokesperson for Afghanistan's supreme leader, said 400 people were killed and 250 injured in this strike.

  • Azerbaijan launches another phase of relocation to liberated territories Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Mar 2026 -- In line with the directives of President Ilham Aliyev, Azerbaijan has launched another phase of the Great Return program, facilitating the relocation of residents to the villages of Childiran, Ashaghi Oratagh, Heyvali, Chapar, and Hasanriz in the Aghdara district; Khojavend village in the Khojavend district; and Horovlu village in the Jabrayil district.

News Reports

  • Russian hybrid threats: four individuals added to EU sanctions list for information manipulation activities European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Mar 2026 -- On 16 March the Council decided to adopt restrictive measures against an additional four individuals responsible for Russia's continued hybrid activities, in particular Foreign Information Manipulation and Interference (FIMI) against the EU and its member states and partners.
  • Keynote speech by HRVP Kaja Kallas at the 2026 Conference on Countering Foreign Information Manipulation and Interference: 'From Insight to Impact' European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Mar 2026

  • HEGCCSG Commends the Efficiency of Security Authorities in the State of Kuwait in Arresting a Hezbollah-Linked Terrorist Cell, and Strongly Condemns Its Plans and Targeting of Kuwait's Security and Stability General Secretariat of the Gulf Cooperation Council 17 Mar 2026

  • EU To Send Team To Druzhba In Bid To Lift Hungarian-Slovak Vetoes RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- The European Union said it will send financial aid and a team of experts to Ukraine with the aim of restoring the flow of oil from the Druzhba pipeline in a bid to lift Hungarian and Slovak obstacles to EU funds for Kyiv and further sanctions on Russia.
  • Statement by the President of the European Commission and the President of the European Council on the Druzhba pipeline* European Commission 17 Mar 2026

  • Meeting with Sberbank Management Board Chairman German Gref President of Russia 17 Mar 2026 -- Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with CEO and Chairman of the Management Board of Sberbank German Gref. The discussion focused on the company's performance last year and current operations.
  • NATO Headquarters hosts discussions about Russia's Shadow Fleet NATO 17 Mar 2026 -- On Tuesday (17 March 2026), delegates and experts from across the Alliance took part in discussions at NATO Headquarters about Russia's Shadow Fleet.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's telephone conversation with Minister of Foreign Affairs of Türkiye Hakan Fidan Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Mar 2026
  • Foreign Minister Tsahkna in Jerusalem: Russia is a threat both in Europe and the Middle East Estonia MFA 17 Mar 2026 -- Foreign Minister Margus Tsahkna was on a visit to Israel today, 17 March, where in meetings with President Isaac Herzog and Foreign Minister Gideon Sa'ar he discussed the latest developments in the Middle East and their links to European security, as well as cooperation between Iran and Russia.
  • Indonesia Discussing Oil Purchases With Russia - Energy Minister Sputnik 17 Mar 2026 -- Indonesia is discussing oil purchases with Russia, Indonesian Energy Minister Bahlil Lahadalia told Sputnik on Tuesday.
  • Moscow's Digital Lockdown: Mobile Internet Outages Hit Russia's Capital RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- Widespread mobile Internet outages have been hitting Moscow for over a week, causing significant disruptions to daily life and raising fears about how far President Vladimir Putin's government will go to tighten its control over online activity.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on March 17, 2026 PRC MofA 17 Mar 2026
  • 'US side has publicly clarified inaccurate reports': Chinese FM spokesperson on media reports that Trump threatened to delay China visit if Beijing does not assist with Hormuz Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- In response to a question from the Global Times reporter regarding media reports claiming that US President Donald Trump said he would postpone his visit to China if China does not help in escort in the Strait of Hormuz, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Tuesday that China has noted that the US side has already publicly clarified the inaccurate media reports by stating that the reports are completely false and emphasizing that the visit is unrelated to the issue of shipping in the Strait of Hormuz.
  • US swiftly denies that any delay in Trump's China visit is tied to request for China to help reopen Hormuz Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- A Financial Times report, which hinted that US President Donald Trump threatened he might postpone his China visit if Beijing does not help the US reopen the Strait of Hormuz, has drawn immediate denials from multiple senior US officials.
  • China, U.S. in communication regarding date of Trump's visit: spokesperson People's Daily 17 Mar 2026 -- China and the United States are in communication with each other regarding the date of President Donald Trump's China visit, and there is no further information available at present, a Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said here on Tuesday.
  • Ministerial meeting of China-Vietnam '3+3' strategic dialogue held, lifting strategic communication, coordination Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- As an important force for peace, stability and justice in the world, China is willing to work with Vietnam and other countries to strengthen coordination within multilateral mechanisms such as APEC, ASEAN and the Lancang Mekong Cooperation, to spare no effort in safeguarding Asia's common home, to jointly advance the construction of an Asia Pacific community, and to set an example in promoting a community with a shared future for mankind, Chinese Foreign Minister Wang Yi told media on Tuesday, after attending the first ministerial meeting of the China-Vietnam "3+3" strategic dialogue on diplomacy, defense and public security in Hanoi, according to a readout released by Chinese Foreign Ministry on Tuesday.
  • Shenzhou-21 taikonauts carry out second extravehicular mission to install space debris shielding Global Times 17 Mar 2026 -- The Shenzhou-21 crew aboard China's orbiting space station on Monday completed their mission's second series of extravehicular activities (EVAs), including installing a space debris protection device for the space station, which a space expert said is a precaution to protect critical systems from debris that cannot be addressed by robotic arms.

  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "PLA Joint Combat Readiness Patrol" ROC MND 17 Mar 2026 -- "Continuously strengthening the combat power of the ROC Armed Forces is the most important task of the Ministry of National Defense!" Minister of National Defense Wellington Koo Li-hsiung said during the general interpellation session at the Legislative Yuan today, expressing the firm stance of the ROC Armed Forces on national defense-related issues.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 17 Mar 2026

  • Japan-U.S.-ROK Public-Private Event to Counter North Korea's Cyber Threats in Europe Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 17 Mar 2026
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Inspects Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at Overseas Military Operations under Construction KCNA 17 Mar 2026 -- Kim Jong Un , general secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and president of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, gave on-site guidance at the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations under construction on March 16.
  • Notice about Convocation of DPRK SPA KCNA 17 Mar 2026 -- The First Session of the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the DPRK will be convened in Pyongyang on March 22, 2026.
  • Press Release of Central Election Committee On Returns of Election for Deputies to 15th SPA of DPRK KCNA 17 Mar 2026 -- The Central Election Committee for the Election of Deputies to the 15th Supreme People's Assembly of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea issued the following press release on March 16:

  • Spokesperson's Press Briefing (March 17, 2026) ROK MFA 17 Mar 2026

  • EU: The Minister's participation in the Foreign Affairs Council (March 17, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 17 Mar 2026
  • Cybersecurity - The EU sanctions several private cyber offensive ecosystem actors (March 17, 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 17 Mar 2026 -- At the March 16th Foreign Affairs Council meeting held in Brussels, member states adopted new listings under the European Union (EU) cyber sanctions regime.
  • The Daily Press Briefing (03/17/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 17 Mar 2026
    • g protects Germany's security interests and enables it to remain an attractive business location.

  • Speech by Foreign Minister Johann Wadephul at the Europe 2026 Conference "Europe at a Turning Point: A Franco-German View of Europe's Sovereignty" Germany Federal Foreign Office 17 Mar 2026

  • Nobel Laureate Byalyatski: Continued Pressure Needed For Political Prisoner Releases In Belarus RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026 -- The potential release of political prisoners in connection with an anticipated visit by a senior US envoy could signal limited progress in negotiations with strongman Aleksandr Lukashenko's government, but repression will persist without sustained international pressure, Nobel Peace Prize laureate Ales Byalyatski has told RFE/RL.

  • Foreign Ministry statement in connection with the escalating situation around Cuba Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 17 Mar 2026

  • Kassym-Jomart Tokayev signs decree giving effect to new Constitution Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 17 Mar 2026 -- President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev on Tuesday signed a decree on measures to implement the Constitution of the Republic of Kazakhstan adopted during the nationwide referendum on March 15, 2026, Qazinform News Agency reported citing the Akorda.

  • Foreign Affairs Council: Press conference by High Representative Kaja Kallas European External Action Service (EEAS) 17 Mar 2026

  • Meeting between the Foreign Ministers of Armenia and Oman Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 17 Mar 2026 -- On March 17, within the framework of the official visit to Oman, in Muscat, Minister of Foreign Affairs of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan had a meeting with Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Sultanate of Oman Sayyid Badr bin Hamad bin Hamood Albusaidi.

  • Wider Europe Briefing: A Crucial Slovenian Vote And A Sticky EU Summit For Ukraine RFE/RL 17 Mar 2026

  • World News in Brief: West Bank displacement, Cuba fuel crisis, Sexual abuse safeguards, New 'humancentric' AI Advocate UN News 17 Mar 2026 -- A new UN human rights report warns that expanding Israeli settlements are driving large-scale displacement of Palestinians across the occupied West Bank.
